big_picture: The Delhi High Court directed a fresh order on Section 244(1A) interest on Income Tax refunds within three months, setting aside an earlier order that denied additional interest. The Delhi High Court ruled that a tribunal erred in directing registration under Sections 12AB and 80G without verifying the genuineness of a trust’s activities. Indian airlines plan to operate 58 evacuation flights to West Asia on Wednesday. Apollo Micro Systems' shares rose 1.5% on March 4 after securing ₹73.32 crore in orders from DRDO and others.

articles.title: Supreme Court Issues Contempt Notice To 51 Delhi Hospitals Over Not Meeting Requirement Of Free Treatment To Weaker Sections
articles.summary: The Supreme Court recently issued contempt notice to 51 hospitals in Delhi over non-compliance with the condition to provide minimum 10 percent IPD (Inpatient Department) and 25 percent OPD (Outpatient Department) for the weaker sections, free of cost. Disallowance of Insurance Expenses Set Aside Because IRDA Limits Do Not Automatically Bar Tax Deduction
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that insurance companies cannot be denied tax deductions for management expenses just because IRDA regulations require them to be reallocated. The decision clarifies that IRDA limits do not automatically override tax laws, ensuring compliance with both regulatory frameworks. This ruling impacts insurance firms' tax planning and financial reporting.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal ruled that reallocating management expenses to the profit and loss account under IRDA regulations does not violate law and therefore cannot justify disallowance under the Income-tax Act. articles.title: Judges Should Not Hesitate To Decide Rightly Even If It Costs Them Elevation Or Displeases Those In Power: Justice Nagarathna
articles.summary: Justice B.V. Nagarathna emphasized that judges must remain free from political pressure, institutional intimidation, or popular demand. She urged judges to decide cases rightly, even if it affects their career or displeases those in power. This underscores the importance of judicial independence in upholding civil liberties and the rule of law in India.
articles.raw_summary: "A judge must be free from political pressure, institutional intimidation, or popular demand," she said.

articles.title: GST Implications on Reimbursement Received on Cancellation of Export Order
articles.summary: Payments received for reimbursing expenses due to cancelled export orders are exempt from GST in India, as no service was provided. This clarification is based on the GST law, which exempts such reimbursements from tax. The ruling helps businesses avoid unnecessary GST liabilities on such transactions.

The road transport ministry's new online system, launched with select banks and NBFCs, eliminates manual visits to RTOs. This seamless process, leveraging RBI's ULI, ensures real-time data exchange, simplifying loan closure for citizens.

articles.title: Under pressure, Karnataka to revise internal quota of SC communities
articles.summary: The Karnataka government is revising its plan to fill 56,432 government posts. The decision was made after pressure from Scheduled Castes communities and ministers. The cabinet will discuss introducing internal reservation within the existing 15% quota for Scheduled Castes.
articles.raw_summary: Karnataka's Congress government is reconsidering its decision to fill 56,432 government posts without internal reservation for Scheduled Castes. Following pressure from SC communities and ministers, the cabinet will discuss introducing pro-rata internal reservation within the existing 15% SC quota.

articles.title: MNRE Sets Emission Thresholds for Green Ammonia and Green Methanol
articles.summary: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) has set emission thresholds and certification frameworks for green ammonia and green methanol as part of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission. This move aims to promote the production and use of green fuels, aligning with the government's broader climate and energy goals. The mission, approved in 2023 with a ₹197.44 billion budget, seeks to boost demand, production, and exports of green hydrogen and related fuels.
articles.raw_summary: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) has notified standards for green ammonia and green methanol under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, establishing emission thresholds and certification frameworks for both fuels. The Union Cabinet had approved the National Green Hydrogen Mission in 2023 to facilitate demand creation, production, utilization, and export of green hydrogen with an initial outlay of ₹197.44 billion (~$2.16 billion).

articles.title: Asset Reconstruction Companies In India: High-Handedness, Judicial Reckoning, And Regulatory Reform
articles.summary: Anyone of us who have appeared before a Debt Recovery Tribunal, a High Court, or the NCLT in a Non-Performing Asset matter has, at some point, encountered an Asset Reconstruction Company on the other side of the record. They arrived with an ambitious mandate: to clean the Indian banking system's distressed debt backlog through professional, non-adjudicatory enforcement, free from the delays that frustrated bank-led recovery for decades. The Narasimham Committee I (1991) and Narasimham Committee

articles.title: Enforcement, yes — but with accountability: Understanding arrest provisions in GST law
articles.summary: The article explains the arrest provisions in India's GST law, emphasizing that while tax evasion cases justify such measures, arrests should be made with accountability. Former CBIC chairman Najib Shah highlights that legal grounds alone do not mandate arrests, ensuring a balanced approach to enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: Given the large-scale detection of tax evasion cases, which have a multiplier effect on the economy and may even fund anti-national activities, arrest provisions are necessary in fiscal laws. However, the Board’s circular makes it clear that the mere existence of legal grounds for arrest does not automatically mandate an arrest, writes former CBIC chairman Najib Shah.

articles.title: Application Rejected as DRT Lacks Jurisdiction Over Recovery Dispute Between Financial Institutions
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the Debt Recovery Tribunal (DRT) lacks jurisdiction over disputes between financial institutions regarding unpaid dues. The court upheld that Section 11 of the SARFAESI Act mandates arbitration for such disputes. This decision impacts how banks and financial institutions resolve recovery disputes, ensuring compliance with the law.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal applied the Supreme Court’s ruling that Section 11 of the SARFAESI Act provides a mandatory arbitration mechanism for disputes between banks or financial institutions involving unpaid dues. This ruling reinforces the legal framework for debt recovery under the SARFAESI Act, ensuring compliance with banking regulations.

articles.title: Indian shipments take costlier, longer route to Europe amid US-Iran tensions
articles.summary: Security concerns around the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea–Suez Canal corridor have prompted major carriers to suspend or restrict transits, extending transit times by 10–20 days and raising freight rates by 40–50% on key India–Europe routes, according to industry executives and insurers.

articles.title: Manufacturing activity surges at fastest pace in four months in February: PMI survey shows
articles.summary: India’s manufacturing activity expanded at its fastest pace in four months in February, powered by strong domestic demand even as export growth softened, S amp;P Global’s PMI survey showed.
articles.raw_summary: India’s manufacturing activity expanded at its fastest pace in four months in February, powered by strong domestic demand even as export growth softened, S amp;P Global’s PMI survey showed.

articles.title: Middle East war unlikely to dent India’s remittance inflows significantly
articles.summary: The US is now the largest source of inward remittances, accounting for 27.7% of such flows into India as of FY24, per latest data from the RBI. The UAE, which was the largest source in FY17, is now the second largest, while nations like Saudi Arabia and Kuwait slipped further down.

articles.title: Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms
articles.summary: The Indian government has revised the BIS rules to speed up product certification and tighten compliance norms. This change places more emphasis on documentation scrutiny and third-party laboratory testing. The move aims to support manufacturing expansion in the country.
articles.raw_summary: Instead of relying heavily on prior on-site assessment, the revised framework places greater emphasis on documentation scrutiny, third-party laboratory testing and post-market surveillance, to support manufacturing expansion.

articles.title: Centre, states collect GST revenue of  ₹1.61 trillion in February after refunds
articles.summary: The Indian government collected ₹1.61 trillion in GST revenue in February, following a rate cut announced in September last year. The government hopes this will boost demand and support higher tax collections. This is a significant government action in the finance sector.
articles.raw_summary: The government has maintained that GST rate cuts announced in September last year would boost demand for goods and services and, over time, support higher tax collections.

articles.title: Will oil prices touch $100? What will be economic impact of US-Israel attack on Iran? Expert weighs in

articles.raw_summary: The US and Israel's attack on Iran may significantly impact global oil prices and inflation, depending on the duration of the conflict and Iranian retaliation. Economists suggest oil could reach $80 per barrel, potentially rising to $100 if supply disruptions occur.

articles.raw_summary: Easing storage norms for industrial gas cylinders draws significance as these cylinders are used to store and transport compressed or liquefied gases for commercial and industrial applications. They are widely used in manufacturing and fabrication.

The government is closely monitoring the situation.

articles.summary: The National Highways Authority of India has awarded a major project to build the Bhubaneswar Bypass. This 111 km, six-lane ring road will connect Rameshwar to Tangi. articles.title: Qatar declares force majeure, gas supplies to Indian industry cut amid Iranian strikes
articles.summary: India faces significant energy disruption as Qatar halts liquefied natural gas deliveries. An Iranian drone strike has forced Qatar to declare force majeure. This halt impacts up to 40 percent of India's gas supplies. Industries are seeing cuts, while CNG and piped gas networks are prioritized. Companies are seeking alternative supplies in a firming spot market.

articles.title: India’s growth is structural, not cyclical
articles.summary: India's economy is growing through investment and reforms, not just stimulus. Public spending on infrastructure is rising, boosting competitiveness. Tax stability and fewer regulations encourage investors. Reforms are being implemented, with a focus on efficient project delivery. Labour laws are being simplified to support manufacturing. Faster dispute resolution is also key.

However, excluding these valuation effects, reserves declined by USD 30.8 billion on a balance of payments basis, indicating underlying external flow pressures.
